page: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

The instance is optimized for network and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m6in.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, placing in the top 25% for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations.

While multi-threaded and cryptographic operations perform exceptionally well, with Passmark CPU encryption tests reaching the top 10% tier, single-core performance remains in the average tier. Additionally, without GPU acceleration, large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Consequently, this instance is best suited for resource-dense general-purpose workloads, including high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with top 25% rankings in multi-core CPU stress tests, software compilation, and memory bandwidth. Cryptographic execution is highly efficient, placing in the top 10% tier. However, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of dedicated hardware acceleration. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for network-heavy workloads, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-core workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, multi-core general purpose instance optimized for demanding network, database, and enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processors with 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong database processing
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
